Method provenance block:
Property: HOMO–LUMO (frontier-orbital) gap, eV
Level of theory: DFT, tier `standard` (B3LYP/def2-TZVP, RI density fitting) in PySCF  (backend as reported)
Geometry provenance: single‐point calculation on the user-supplied Cartesian geometry in /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/frontier-orbitals-validation/pyridine/pyridine.xyz; no geometry optimisation performed (notation: DFT//user-provided)
Solvent / environment: gas phase (no implicit solvation)
Charge & multiplicity: 0, 1 (closed-shell singlet)
SCF convergence & caveats: SCF converged in 10 cycles; no engine warnings were reported. No implicit‐solvent or tier downgrades triggered.  No warnings reported.
Software: PySCF (version string as in engine banner; density fitting on by default).

Result
The B3LYP/def2-TZVP (RI) gas-phase HOMO–LUMO gap of pyridine (charge 0, singlet) is:

    6.1398131 eV

Additional frontier-orbital data returned by the chemkit frontier-orbitals skill include:

• LUMO (index 21) energy = –1.112786 eV
• Selected nearby orbitals: HOMO−1 = –7.4731156 eV, HOMO−2 = –8.0425195 eV; LUMO+1 = –0.67231536 eV, LUMO+2 = 1.1781069 eV.

Live output log: the skill streamed PySCF stdout/stderr to its standard <subcommand>_<timestamp>.out log in the caller’s working directory while running. (Exact path not included in the JSON, but is present alongside the run artifacts; please let me know if you would like it surfaced.)

Files and reproducibility

• Command as executed (from `cli_invocation`):
  /home/erobles/chem-skills/mcp_server/chemkit_engine/cli.py frontier --method dft --tier standard /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/frontier-orbitals-validation/pyridine/pyridine.xyz --out /home/erobles/chem-skills/.fidelity_scratch/tmpv_vf9odh/live.json
